name: Sendero Rinconada to Ruidero bridge (PR-TO 29)
region_intro: Hiking trails around La Puebla De Montalbán traverse a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river plains, and dramatic geological formations. The region is notably defined by the Barrancas de Burujón, impressive clay-cut ravines along the Tagus River, and extends into the Montes de Toledo. These features offer varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around La Puebla De Montalbán The most popular hiking route is Barrancas de Burujón — circular, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the unique ravines of Burujón. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barrancas de Burujón Ecological Trail, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route offers an ecological perspective of the striking clay formations and the Tagus River. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Montalbán from the Melque set, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through historical surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around La Puebla De Montalbán is defined by the impressive Barrancas de Burujón, the rolling hills of the Montes de Toledo, and scenic river plains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in La Puebla De Montalbán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 590 hikers have used komoot to explore La Puebla De Montalbán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around La Puebla de Montalbán, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more moderate excursions through diverse terrain.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape, including the dramatic clay-cut ravines of the &lt;strong&gt;Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/strong&gt;, rolling hills of the &lt;strong&gt;Montes de Toledo&lt;/strong&gt;, and scenic river plains along the Tagus. The &lt;strong&gt;Castrejón Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; also adds a picturesque element to many views.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 10 easy hiking trails in the area. A great option for an accessible experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343918425/barrancas-de-burujon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 3.4 miles (5.5 km) and takes about 1 hour 35 minutes to complete, exploring the unique ravines.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Yes, several circular walks are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343918425/barrancas-de-burujon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy option. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3961839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Burujón – Enebros Viewpoint loop from Río Las Cabreras&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints to visit?
A: The region is famous for the &lt;strong&gt;Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/strong&gt;. You can enjoy breathtaking panoramic views from several points, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cambrón&lt;/a&gt;, both offering spectacular sights of the ravines and the Castrejón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any historical sites that can be explored while hiking?
A: Yes, the area combines natural beauty with rich history. You can explore historical surroundings on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018816470/castillo-de-montalban-from-the-melque-set" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montalbán from the Melque set&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the historic Montalbán Castle.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the impressive geological formations of the Barrancas de Burujón and the varied terrain that offers both scenic river plains and rolling hills.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, there is a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;parking area at Las Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/a&gt;, making it convenient to access trails in that popular natural monument.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, there is one difficult route available for those seeking a greater challenge. For a longer moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018808927/sendero-rinconada-to-ruidero-bridge-pr-to-29" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Rinconada to Ruidero bridge (PR-TO 29)&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 19 km long.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Castrejón Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. Many trails and viewpoints within the Barrancas de Burujón area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoints of Las Barrancas and Castrejón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offer stunning vistas of the reservoir, adding to the picturesque scenery.

Q: What is the Barrancas de Burujón Ecological Trail?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018226857/barrancas-de-burujon-ecological-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón Ecological Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) path that provides an in-depth ecological perspective of the striking clay formations and the Tagus River, taking approximately 2 hours to complete.
tips: The path from La Rinconada to the Ruidero bridge follows the course of the Torcón river and allows you to reach the castle of Montalbán, a fairly long route that can be shortened while allowing you to enjoy this beautiful landscape.
